---
title: "Source Guide: Subspaces, Basis, and Dimension"
description: "A curated source guide for learning subspaces, basis, and dimension from first pass through research bridge."
page-type: source-guide
status: published
update-status: current
level: foundation
topic: linear-algebra
prerequisites:
  - /topics/foundation/linear-algebra/concepts/subspaces-basis-dimension.qmd
learning-goals:
  - "Choose the right source for subspace language at different depths."
  - "Separate durable basis/dimension references from current low-dimensional bridges."
  - "See which sources belong to the concept, application, and research pages in this package."
applications:
  - dimensionality-reduction
  - regression
keywords:
  - source guide
  - subspace
  - basis
  - dimension
maintainer: mtuann
last-reviewed: 2026-04-24
source-check-date: 2026-04-24
review-cadence: semiannual
date-modified: last-modified
draft: false
stable-sources:
  - "MIT 18.06SC Basis and Dimension"
  - "Stanford VMLS"
  - "Hefferon, Linear Algebra"
current-sources:
  - "Deep learning, transformers and graph neural networks: a linear algebra perspective (2025)"
---

## How To Use This Guide

Use one layer at a time:

- `First pass` for honest subspace language
- `Second pass` for proof and exercise depth
- `Paper bridge` for seeing why low-dimensional structure matters in practice

## Stable Core

- [MIT 18.06SC: Basis and Dimension](https://live.ocw.mit.edu/courses/18-06sc-linear-algebra-fall-2011/resources/basis-and-dimension-1/) - `First pass` - best official source for the span/basis/dimension chain. Checked `2026-04-24`.
- [Introduction to Applied Linear Algebra – Vectors, Matrices, and Least Squares](https://web.stanford.edu/~boyd/vmls/) - `First pass` - strong applied framing once you want low-dimensional modeling language. Checked `2026-04-24`.
- [Hefferon, Linear Algebra](https://hefferon.net/linearalgebra/) - `Second pass` - stronger self-study source for proof and exercise depth. Checked `2026-04-24`.

## Current Bridge

- [Deep learning, transformers and graph neural networks: a linear algebra perspective](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s11075-025-02218-2) - `Second pass` - current bridge from low-dimensional structure to representation learning. Checked `2026-04-24`.

## Paper Bridge

- [A Survey: Potential Dimensionality Reduction Methods](https://arxiv.org/abs/2502.11036) - `Paper bridge` - best current first bridge from subspace language to method families. Checked `2026-04-24`.
- [Low-rank approximations](https://nlp.stanford.edu/IR-book/html/htmledition/low-rank-approximations-1.html) - `Second pass` - useful next stop once you want to see how low-dimensional subspaces become practical approximation models in a concrete application area. Checked `2026-04-24`.

## Recommended Reading Paths

### Path 1: First serious understanding

1. MIT 18.06SC Basis and Dimension
2. [Subspaces, Basis, and Dimension](../concepts/subspaces-basis-dimension.qmd)
3. Hefferon for more exercises

### Path 2: From basis language to applications

1. [Subspaces, Basis, and Dimension](../concepts/subspaces-basis-dimension.qmd)
2. [Low-Dimensional Subspace Models](../applications/low-dimensional-subspace-models.qmd)
3. [Computation Lab: Basis and Column Space Geometry](../labs/basis-and-column-space-geometry.qmd)

### Path 3: Bridge to dimensionality reduction

1. [Subspaces, Basis, and Dimension](../concepts/subspaces-basis-dimension.qmd)
2. [SVD and Low-Rank Approximation](../concepts/svd-and-low-rank-approximation.qmd)
3. [Dimensionality Reduction as Subspace Modeling](../paper-lab/dimensionality-reduction-as-subspace-modeling.qmd)

## Sources Checked Online

All links above were checked on `2026-04-24`.

The verification pass included:

- official MIT OCW pages
- official Stanford or author-maintained textbook pages
- current arXiv survey page for dimensionality reduction
- current journal page for the 2025 linear-algebra perspective survey
